svg.text: type parameters — placeholders resolved at each call site

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Rust type parameter T in detail with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Rust type parameter U in detail with examples')
sendPrompt('Explain Rust type parameter F: Fn(T)->U in depth')

svg.text: where clause — capability contracts the compiler enforces

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain T: Clone + Debug bounds in Rust')
sendPrompt('Explain U: Display + Default bounds in Rust')

svg.text: Fn trait hierarchy — why Fn and not FnOnce or FnMut

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain FnOnce trait in Rust — when is it used?')
sendPrompt('Explain FnMut trait in Rust — when is it used?')
sendPrompt('Explain Fn trait — why is it the strongest bound?')

svg.text: iterator chain — lazy type-threading, nothing runs until .collect()
each adapter is a zero-sized struct wrapping the previous — no allocation until .collect()
type inference — compiler solves T, U, F from call site context
monomorphization — compiler stamps out a concrete function per call site
two call sites → two concrete fns → zero runtime dispatch overhead
each compiles to machine code as if hand-written for that exact type pair
